description:
You are working as a junior log-analysis engineer.  
    The senior analyst has placed a raw DNS traffic capture at:

        /home/user/logs/dns_traffic.log

    1. Study that file without altering it.  
       • Each log line is already whitespace-separated and has the structure  
         TIMESTAMP␠HOSTNAME␠IPADDRESS  
         Example:  
         2023-03-01T12:00:01Z␠example.com␠93.184.216.34  

    2. Produce a one-line summary for every distinct HOSTNAME showing how many **unique** IP addresses were seen for that hostname in the whole file.  
       • Count every *different* IP only once per hostname.  
       • Do not include the timestamp in your summary.  
       • Ignore any possible blank lines (there are none right now, but your command should handle that gracefully).

    3. Write the result to the file

        /home/user/output/dns_hostname_unique_ips.log

       Requirements for that file:  
       • One summary per line, sorted in pure lexicographical (ASCII) order by HOSTNAME.  
       • Each line must contain exactly TWO fields separated by **one horizontal tab (U+0009)** and nothing else:  
         HOSTNAME<TAB>UNIQUE_IP_COUNT  
       • Terminate the file with a single trailing newline (the very last character must be “\n”).  
       • Example of correct final format (values will differ):  
         alpha.example<TAB>1\n  
         beta.example<TAB>3\n  

    4. If /home/user/output does not yet exist, create it.

    5. When finished, you can `cat /home/user/output/dns_hostname_unique_ips.log` to show the file’s contents on screen, but the **existence and exact contents** of that file are what the automated test will inspect.

    Hint for an efficient solution (not mandatory): one short pipeline using `awk`, `sort`, and `uniq -c` is enough.
truth:
Initial files/directories already present before the agent starts
    ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    Directory: /home/user/logs

    File: /home/user/logs/dns_traffic.log
    Exact contents (every line ends with UNIX newline “\n”):

2023-03-01T12:00:01Z example.com 93.184.216.34
2023-03-01T12:00:02Z example.com 93.184.216.34
2023-03-01T12:00:03Z localhost 127.0.0.1
2023-03-01T12:00:04Z example.net 93.184.216.34
2023-03-01T12:00:05Z example.com 93.184.216.35
2023-03-01T12:00:06Z example.net 93.184.216.34

    (There is a final “\n” after the last line.)

    What the agent **must** create
    ──────────────────────────────
    Directory: /home/user/output  (if not present)

    File: /home/user/output/dns_hostname_unique_ips.log
    Required exact contents (each “↵” represents a UNIX newline):

example.com<TAB>2↵
example.net<TAB>1↵
localhost<TAB>1↵

    • <TAB> above is the literal single tab character (0x09).
    • The file ends with the final newline shown after “localhost<TAB>1”.
name: task_000000_8ea054e1
